pattern: Dynamic programming over suffixes where each suffix can either be deleted whole or shortened by a prefix that matches the block right after it, using precomputed longest-common-prefix lengths.
hints: Define the answer for each starting index as the best number of operations to clear the suffix from there onward.
From position i you may always finish in one move, or remove i letters when s[i:i+len] equals s[i+len:i+2*len], jumping to that later position plus one.
Precompute lcp[i][j] = length of the common prefix of suffixes i and j so the equality check is O(1), then fill dp from the back.

edge_inputs.description: all identical letters peel one at a time
repeated prefix then no more matches
mixed run lengths force careful dp choices
no removable prefix anywhere, only whole-string deletion
doubled string deletes prefix then whole
greedy small-i choice is not always optimal

stress.description: random strings over a tiny alphabet to maximize matching prefixes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    alpha = 'ab'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: two-letter alphabet creates many equal blocks, stressing the dp branches
